No sign-in is required. The app has no account, subscription, in-app purchase, wallet, or payment flow.

HNS DANE Browser uses WKWebView for rendering and an authenticated loopback proxy at 127.0.0.1 for app traffic. It does not install a VPN or change the device's system proxy.

Ordinary web test:
1. Launch the app. When "Enter an address to begin" appears, enter https://denuoweb.com/work/hns-dane-browser and tap Go.
2. Confirm the page loads and the security label shows the WebPKI path.

Handshake test:
1. Enter https://denuoweb/ and tap Go.
2. The bundled Handshake header snapshot allows immediate proof-backed resolution while foreground header sync continues.
3. Confirm the security label identifies the resolver and TLS validation path.
4. Tap the Settings gear button to inspect HNS resolution, HNS sync, Clear resolver cache, Resolver trace, HNS proof details, Privacy policy, and Source code.

Compatibility note: some review networks block Handshake peer traffic or direct authoritative DNS. In Compatibility mode, the app may use its HNS DNS-over-HTTPS fallback and labels that path. Strict Handshake mode disables that fallback. The experimental P2P DNS relay is enabled by default on iOS; DNSSEC and proof validation remain local.

Cleartext HTTP pages are marked insecure. No reviewer-supplied personal data or credentials are needed. The two test URLs above are developer-controlled.

Privacy policy: https://denuoweb.com/work/hns-dane-browser/privacy
Source code: https://github.com/Denuo-Web/hns-dane-browser
